mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in retail sales indicates robust consumer demand, which translates into higher corporate earnings expectations and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as investors price in future revenue growth.
failure_condition: If the 20-day forward returns for S&P 500 do not show a statistically significant positive correlation with RSAFS month-over-month growth over any 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in the unemployment rate above a critical threshold signals a weakening labor market, leading to reduced consumer spending and lower corporate profits, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 60-day forward returns are positive or flat (average return < 0.1%) in more than 40% of instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over three months.

mechanism_narrative: When inflation (CPIAUCSL) is high and the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is also high, it indicates a restrictive monetary policy environment aimed at curbing inflation, which typically dampens economic activity and corporate earnings, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are consistently positive (average > 0.5%) when both CPIAUCSL is above 200 and FEDFUNDS is above 5.0% for any 12-month period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic expansion, increasing industrial production signals robust manufacturing activity and corporate profitability, which positively influences short-term S&P 500 returns over a 5-day horizon as investors react to strong economic data.
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative during periods identified as economic expansion when INDPRO growth exceeds 0.2% month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: A significant increase in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market and economic growth, boosting consumer confidence and spending, which leads to positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in more than 35% of instances where PAYEMS increases by over 150,000 month-over-month.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent growth in housing starts (HOUST) reflects strong consumer and business confidence, indicating future economic activity and investment, which supports positive S&P 500 returns over a longer 60-day horizon as these trends materialize.
failure_condition: If the rolling 60-day average S&P 500 return does not show a positive correlation with the 3-month average growth rate of HOUST over any 3-year period.

mechanism_narrative: When the Federal Funds Rate (FEDFUNDS) is stable or declining while retail sales (RSAFS) are growing, it suggests an accommodative monetary environment supporting strong consumer demand, which is bullish for short-term S&P 500 returns over 5 trading days.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS change < 0.1% month-over-month AND RSAFS growth > 0.5% month-over-month
failure_condition: If the average 5-day forward return for S&P 500 is negative when FEDFUNDS is stable/declining and RSAFS is growing, over any 2-year period.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of economic slowdown or recession, a decline in GDP (GDPC1) signals weakening corporate revenues and profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as investors anticipate further economic contraction.
failure_condition: If S&P 500 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances where GDPC1 shows negative quarter-over-quarter growth during a declared recession.
